rationale: The two papers share a broad domain (government/agency regulation and consumer responses in health-related consumption), so the 2017 JMR article could plausibly serve as prior evidence on regulatory effects on demand. However, the citing paper’s stated core mechanisms are political ideology and reactance, while the cited paper focuses on FTC false-claim enforcement and market demand, and the provided evidence contains only a DOI match with no in-text citation context.
